channel.title: thehackersbrain | Gaurav Raj - Security Researcher
channel.description: I'm Gaurav (thehackersbrain), Founder of Cybercraft Labs — Offensive Security Researcher and Software Engineer with close to a decade (8+ years) of experience.

item.title: Sliver C2 — Active Directory Hacking Cheatsheet
item.description: Comprehensive Sliver C2 reference for Active Directory offensive operations. Covers setup, implant generation, armory tools, AD enumeration, Kerberos attacks, lateral movement, domain dominance, and OPSEC.

item.title: Create a Hacking Lab on Cloud using Docker for free
item.description: Learn how to create a free hacking lab on the cloud using Docker and Kali Linux. This guide covers setting up a secure, free environment for pentest and cybersecurity experiments.

item.title: Create an Image Steganography Program in Python
item.description: Discover how to create an image steganography tool in Python. Learn to securely hide and extract data within PNG images using PIL and cryptography modules.

item.title: Unveiling the Dangers of Insecure Deserialization!
item.description: Insecure deserialization in web apps is a critical vuln that allows attackers to exploit serialized objects for code execution.

item.title: Comprehensive Guide to Finding and Exploiting SSRF Vulnerabilities
item.description: Explore an in-depth guide on finding and exploiting Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities.

item.title: Navigating the Cloud - The Crucial Role of Security Researchers in Understanding Development and Deployment Process (Cloud Hacking)
item.description: Explore the critical role of security researchers in understanding development and deployment processes within cloud environments. Dive into real-world scenarios, expert insights, and how Cybercraft Labs Pvt Ltd. empowers researchers for cybersecurity excellence. Read now!

item.title: The Importance of Secure Coding Practices - Cybercraft Labs
item.description: Secure coding is the practice of writing code that is designed to be secure from the outset. It involves following a set of best practices and guidelines that are designed to prevent common security vulnerabilities from being introduced into your code.
